content.full_text:
May 31, 1984

James C. Gross
Nielson, Hodgson, Parrinello and Mueller
1225 Eighth Street, Suite 550
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Advice Letter No. A-84-143

Dear Jim,

This letter is to confirm my telephone advice to you on May 25, 1984. You stated that a political action committee's board of directors contains legislators who comprise a minority of the board. Your question was:

Is the committee "controlled" by the legislators within the meaning of Government Code Section 82016?1/

DISCUSSION

Section 82016 provides that a candidate "controls" a committee if "he ... has a significant influence on the actions or decisions of the committee." A legislator who is a voting member of the governing board of a committee ordinarily will be considered to have a significant influence over the actions or decisions of a committee. Therefore, unless there are some unusual circumstances, the legislators on the board are "controlling candidates" who should comply with 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18430 (copy enclosed). For clarity, they may want to indicate on the committee's Statement of Organization and

1/ Hereinafter all statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise stated.

campaign statements that they are part of the committee's board of directors.

Very truly yours,

Janis Shank McLean
Counsel
Legal Division

JSM:plh
Enclosure
